design_rationale: Color system built from the strategy brief's 'Sentinel' archetype and visual direction: calm authority, restrained confidence, and precision. Deep navy anchors the brand in trust and technology. A single electric teal accent provides energy and modernity without consumer playfulness. Warm neutrals prevent clinical coldness while maintaining professional clarity.

deep_navy.name: Parq Navy
deep_navy.role: Primary brand color — dominant in headers, hero sections, primary UI surfaces, and logo background

electric_teal.name: Parq Teal
electric_teal.role: Primary accent — CTAs, links, data highlights, interactive elements, and the 'AI' in the logo

accent_blue.name: Parq Blue
accent_blue.role: Secondary accent — charts, secondary buttons, supporting data visualizations, hover states

off_black.name: Ink
off_black.role: Primary text color on light backgrounds

dark_gray.name: Slate
dark_gray.role: Secondary text, captions, and muted labels

light_gray.name: Mist
light_gray.role: Card backgrounds, secondary surfaces, and subtle fills

off_white.name: Cloud
off_white.role: Page backgrounds — warmer than pure white to avoid clinical feel

success.name: Success Green
success.role: Positive states — confirmations, online status, compliance indicators

warning.name: Warning Amber
warning.role: Caution states — expiring sessions, approaching limits, review needed

error.name: Error Red
error.role: Error and destructive states — failures, violations, critical alerts. Used sparingly and only for system states, never as a brand color.

info.name: Info Blue
info.role: Informational states — tooltips, notices, neutral notifications

dark_mode.description: Dark mode palette for dashboard UI and potential dark website sections. Built on the navy foundation with adjusted contrast levels.

teal_on_dark.note: Slightly brighter teal for sufficient contrast on dark surfaces

blue_on_dark.note: Lightened blue for dark surface readability

accessibility.minimum_requirement: AA (4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text)
accessibility.target: AAA where possible (7:1 for normal text, 4.5:1 for large text)

contrast_pairings.use_case: Primary body text on page background
Primary body text on card background
Light text on dark hero sections and headers
Teal accent text/elements on dark backgrounds
AVOID — Teal on light backgrounds fails contrast. Use only for decorative/large graphic elements, never for text.
contrast_pairings.mitigation: Use darkened teal variant #008C83 (hex) on light backgrounds for text (achieves 4.6:1)

contrast_pairings.use_case: Teal links and text on light backgrounds (accessible variant)
Primary text in dark mode cards

accessible_teal_variant.name: Teal Accessible
accessible_teal_variant.role: Use this darkened teal for all text rendered on light backgrounds. The primary Parq Teal (#00C2B7) is reserved for use on dark backgrounds and large decorative elements only.

color_proportions.dominant_70: Parq Navy (#0A1628) and neutral palette — backgrounds, large surfaces, text containers
color_proportions.secondary_25: Cloud (#F5F6F8) and Mist (#E8EAF0) — content areas, cards, breathing space
color_proportions.accent_5: Parq Teal (#00C2B7) and Parq Blue (#2E7CF6) — CTAs, highlights, data points, interactive elements

by_purpose.page_backgrounds: Cloud (#F5F6F8) for light mode. Dark Background (#070D1A) for dark mode.
by_purpose.hero_sections: Parq Navy (#0A1628) with Cloud text. Teal accent for key metrics or CTA.
by_purpose.primary_cta_buttons: Parq Teal (#00C2B7) background with Parq Navy (#0A1628) text — provides excellent contrast (8.6:1).
by_purpose.secondary_cta_buttons: Outlined with Parq Navy border on light backgrounds, or Parq Blue on dark backgrounds.
by_purpose.links: Teal Accessible (#008C83) on light backgrounds. Parq Teal (#00C2B7) on dark backgrounds.
by_purpose.data_visualizations: Teal as primary data color, Blue as secondary. Use neutral grays for baselines and axes.
by_purpose.navigation: Parq Navy background with Cloud text. Active state uses Parq Teal.
by_purpose.form_elements: Stone (#8B92A7) borders, Ink (#1A1F2E) input text, Parq Teal focus ring.
by_purpose.status_indicators: Use semantic colors exclusively — green for compliance/online, amber for warnings, red for violations/errors.

brand_consistency_rules: Never use Parq Teal (#00C2B7) for body text on light backgrounds — use Teal Accessible (#008C83) instead
Parq Navy is always the dominant visual element — teal and blue are accents, not co-primaries
Semantic colors are functional only — never use error red or warning amber as decorative colors
Dark mode surfaces must use the navy-based dark palette, not pure black (#000000)
Maintain warm undertones in all neutrals — no pure gray (#808080) or pure white (#FFFFFF)
When teal and blue appear together (e.g., charts), teal takes visual priority as the primary accent

red_as_brand_color.reason: Connotes punishment, aggression, and violation — contradicts the brand's positioning as a compliance partner rather than an enforcer. Reserve red exclusively for system error states.

bright_orange.reason: Reads as consumer-facing, playful, and startup-coded. Conflicts with the professional enterprise positioning.

purple.reason: Signals creativity, luxury, or consumer lifestyle — none of which align with Parq's pragmatic, operations-focused brand.

multi_color_palettes.reason: Using 4+ colors dilutes brand authority and creates visual noise. The strategy calls for restrained confidence — a tight palette communicates discipline and focus.
multi_color_palettes.exception: Data visualizations requiring 4+ categories may use extended tints/shades of teal and blue, never introducing new hue families

pure_black.reason: Pure black (#000000) is harsh on screens and creates too-stark contrast. Use Parq Navy or Ink instead.

pure_white.reason: Pure white (#FFFFFF) feels clinical and cold. Use Cloud (#F5F6F8) or Off-White for backgrounds.
pure_white.exception: Text on dark backgrounds may use #F0F1F4 or #F5F6F8 for maximum readability

extended_tints_and_shades.description: Extended scale for data visualizations and UI components that need gradation within the brand palette.

css_custom_properties.description: Ready-to-use CSS custom property definitions for implementation.
